title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Low-Resource Question Answering
abstract: We study how retrieval-augmented generation behaves when the target language has little training data, and show that multilingual dense retrievers close most of the gap.

title: Dense Retrieval Benchmarks for German Scientific Text
abstract: A benchmark suite for dense retrieval over German-language scientific abstracts, comparing multilingual embedding models against translate-then-retrieve pipelines.

title: Hallucination Detection in Grounded Dialogue Systems
abstract: We propose an entailment-based method to flag unsupported claims in dialogue responses that cite retrieved documents.

title: Weak Supervision for Misinformation Detection on Social Media
abstract: Labelling functions derived from fact-checking archives train competitive misinformation classifiers without manual annotation.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Fraud Detection in Payment Networks
abstract: We model transaction streams as temporal graphs and show that message-passing architectures detect collusive fraud patterns earlier than feature-based baselines.

title: Explainable Ranking Models for Legal Document Search
abstract: A study of feature attribution methods applied to learning-to-rank models in a legal retrieval setting, with a user study among law students.

title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Medical Image Analysis
abstract: We evaluate federated averaging across five hospital datasets for tumour segmentation and quantify the privacy-utility trade-off under differential privacy.

title: Self-Supervised Pretraining for Histopathology Slide Classification
abstract: Contrastive pretraining on unlabelled whole-slide images improves downstream cancer subtype classification with an order of magnitude fewer labels.

title: Uncertainty Quantification in Clinical Risk Prediction Models
abstract: Conformal prediction gives distribution-free coverage guarantees for ICU risk scores and exposes silent failure modes of deep ensembles.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Energy Management in Smart Grids
abstract: A model-based RL controller reduces peak load in a simulated district heating network while respecting hard safety constraints.

title: Sample-Efficient Robot Skill Learning from Human Demonstrations
abstract: Combining behavioural cloning with offline RL lets a manipulation robot acquire kitchen tasks from under one hour of demonstrations.

title: Causal Inference for Observational Health Data: A Tutorial and Case Study
abstract: We walk through target trial emulation on Swiss claims data and compare propensity-score and doubly robust estimators.

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Models for Small-Area Disease Mapping
abstract: A spatial Bayesian framework estimates cantonal disease rates with credible intervals that remain calibrated in sparsely populated regions.

title: Large Language Models as Annotators for Political Text Analysis
abstract: We audit LLM-generated labels for framing and stance in parliamentary debates and find systematic biases that vary by language and party family.

title: Measuring Polarisation in Multilingual Online Discourse
abstract: A cross-lingual embedding approach tracks affective polarisation across German, French and Italian Swiss news comments over a decade.

title: Deep Learning Surrogates for Climate Model Downscaling
abstract: Diffusion-based super-resolution reconstructs kilometre-scale precipitation fields from coarse climate simulations over the Alps.

title: Remote Sensing of Alpine Glacier Retreat with Sentinel Time Series
abstract: A change-detection pipeline over satellite imagery quantifies glacier area loss in the Swiss Alps between 2015 and 2024.

title: Algorithmic Fairness in University Admission Decisions
abstract: We formalise competing fairness criteria for admission scoring and show empirically that post-processing repairs shift rather than remove disparities.

title: Neural Machine Translation for Swiss German Dialects
abstract: We collect a parallel corpus of Swiss German dialect variants and fine-tune multilingual translation models, analysing transfer across dialect regions.

title: Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage for National Health Registries
abstract: Bloom-filter encodings with differential privacy enable linking cancer registry records across cantons without exchanging identifiers.
